Title: Indoor Climbing MANCHESTER - Beginners welcome!
Summary:
This event is intended as a safe space for the LGBTQ+ community; everyone will be warmly welcomed
Join us for an evening of climbing in one of the UK's top climbing centres. Whether you are new to climbing or a seasoned climber, this centre offers something for everyone with a huge selection of routes including top rope, lead and auto belay.
Prefer to boulder? That's well catered for as well, with an extensive modern bouldering area ensuring there is plenty to keep you busy, or you can swap between climbing and bouldering on the same night.
Climbing is the main focus of the event, but this is also a great chance to meet other members and build lasting friendships. Many Outdoor Lads members join through climbing, so if you're new to the group and want to know more, come along and chat over a cuppa during the tea and cake break.
What to expect on your first climb with us?
If you are an experienced climber and have passed the centre's proficiency test, we'll pair you up with a climbing buddy and you'll be out exploring the centre in no time.
If you have never climbed before or think you could do with a refresher, we offer supervised climbing sessions with one of our leaders, subject to availability. That session will focus on introducing you to climbing, making sure you get plenty of time on the wall whilst also showing you how to belay safely. Due to the centre's rules, we are limited in the number of climbers the leader is able to supervise in each session so if you think you may need a supervised session,
please contact the leader before signing up to make sure there is enough space.
As a general guide, if you answer "no" to any of the following questions, you should opt for a supervised session:
Can you fit a climbing harness correctly?
Can you tie into a rope using the correct knots?
Can you belay and lower down another climber correctly and confidently?
After a few supervised sessions, you should be ready to complete the centre's proficiency test and once that has been passed, you'll be able to climb with the rest of the group.
Everything else you need to know
You will need to register with Big Depot before your first visit to the centre but you only need to do this once. If you are an experienced climber, you can save time and do this online
here
, selecting the 18+ unsupervised option. This also applies if you just want to boulder, just select the relevant options during the registration. If you are a beginner and require a supervised session, the leader will help register you when you are at the centre.
Photos provided by Sieh and Dexmonster.
Difficulty & Distance: There is a range of routes of varying difficulty, but there should be something to suit everybody

Title: Bouldering MANCHESTER - Beginners welcome
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads For An Evening of INDOOR BOULDERING in Manchester!
This event is intended as a safe space for the LGBTQ+ community; everyone will be warmly welcomed
Bouldering is a form of climbing that focuses on shorter "problems" emphasising technique, balance, body positioning, and sometimes power, rather than long, high routes. Without the need for ropes, harnesses, and belaying, bouldering is an accessible activity for newcomers and is often considered the "purest" form of climbing.
Bouldering offers a wide range of benefits, including:
Physical:
Improved strength, tone, balance, power, and coordination.
Mental:
Enhanced problem-solving, focus, concentration, and body awareness.
Emotional:
Relaxation, a sense of achievement, and a feeling of mind-body connection.
Bouldering is also a fantastic way to meet new people and build lasting friendships, as it's an inherently social sport where climbers share tips and strategies to conquer the routes.
New to Bouldering?
If you're new to bouldering, it is recommended that you have a guided session from the event leader to learn the basics of safe bouldering. The leader can then introduce you to the rest of the group where you can refine your newly learnt skills in a social environment.
Experienced Boulderer?
If you’re new to climbing with ODL, the leader will help to introduce you to the rest of the group so you can explore the centre together.
You are responsible for your own safety and actions while bouldering. Please use your own judgement when accepting advice from OutdoorLads Volunteers or other experienced climbers, regardless of your bouldering experience.
Ready to boulder?
You will need to sign up on the ODL event page. We would ask you to do this each time so that the leader has access to important information
If you are not already registered with Rock Over Climbing, please do so
here
to save time when you arrive. You only need to do this once.
When you arrive at the centre you will need to pay for entry and hire climbing shoes if you don't own a pair. ODL members benefit from the concessionary rate so please mention this when paying.
Get bouldering! You’ll find most of the group already bouldering so feel free to join them. If you’re new to bouldering with ODL, the leader will be looking out for you so if you spot them, please go speak with them first.
Any questions?
If you have any questions, please contact the leader directly.
Photos : Copyright granted to OutdoorLads by
Sieh
and joenellist.
Difficulty & Distance: Suitable for all abilities. If you're new to bouldering, just let the event leader know and we'll get you started!

Title: Glentress Long Weekend
Summary:
Come and join our friends at OutMTB for a long weekend of off-road biking fun at Scotlands premier mountain biking centre.
Glentress itself is a huge trail centre, with a mixture of more traditional forest trails with lots of fun, techy features and plenty of newer, faster-flow trails.  Nearby Innerleithen also has some fantastic official trails and there are also some smashing natural trails.  The plan for the weekend is to combine some of all of these elements, although plans might change a little when we see what the weather brings!
Please note: OutMTB is aimed at the full LGBT community, so there is a chance that there may be members of other genders on this event.
This is a mountain biking trip, so a well serviced mountain bike will be required - front suspension minimum.
Current plans are:
Saturday 29th - Glentress trails.  Intermediate level ride, mixture of blue and red trails.  Likely around 25km, 800m elevation, 2-4 hours riding.
Sunday 30th - Gypsy Glen.  This is a nearby route of natural MTB trails.  This means fewer features like berms and jumps, but more unpredictable things like rocks and roots.  Comes with some excellent views!  This is also an intermediate type ride, 2-4 hours duration, 25km distance and 700m of elevation.
Sunday 31st - Innerleithen red trails: again 2-4 hours riding, 25km and around 800m elevation.
We will meet at the Glentress carpark for the Saturday event.  Should you wish to only attend the Sunday/Monday then get in touch and we will arrange a meeting point.
There are lots of accommodation options in the area.  I will be sharing a lodge at Glentress, there are also pods and camping spots and the lower part of the area, as well as plenty of hotel options either at Glentress or nearby Peebles. Bike hire is available at Glentress.
Photos:
by Marcus8
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - -
Want to keep in touch with what's going on in Scotland?
Join our Telegram
chat
and
notification
groups, and bookmark our upcoming events list via our
Scottish Calendar
.
Difficulty & Distance: A range of biking will be on offer, not super technical but things can be unpredictable.  A reasonable amount of distance and climbing.

Title: Outwood Bound
Summary:
Join Paul on this leisurely bike ride from Manchester to Bolton and back.
Everyone (including beginners) is welcome. No one will get left behind. We'll ride at a gentle pace of 8 to 10 miles per hour for about 3 hours, but the event timing is longer to allow for the natural stops and starts of the ride, plus a short lunch stop in Bolton.
There are frequent trains between Bolton and Manchester, so there's the option of leaving/joining the ride in Bolton if you fancy a shorter cycle of less than 2 hours. If joining us from Bolton, please book onto the event as usual, then message our ride leader Paul for details of where to meet and the approximate meeting time.
Route information
Starting from Central Manchester, we will ride along National Cycle Network (NCN) Route 55 to Bolton. This follows roads with cycle lanes as far as Eccles (or there's a slightly longer off-road option along canal paths and dedicated cycle paths). We'll then use the quiet roads in the leafy suburb of Ellesmere Park to get to Monton. There we join the former Monton Green to Bolton Railway line, known as the Roe Green loop line which keeps us mainly off road until we reach the centre of Bolton.
Our return leg continues mainly off road to NCN Route 6 and the Outwood Trail, another former rail ay line running between Radclliffe and Prestwich. After passing through Drinkwater Park to the outskirts of Salford, we will keep following NCN Route 6 (which has on and off road options) back to the centre of Manchester.
Although much of the route is traffic free or on quieter roads, we may encounter the odd busy road. There will also be some fairly gentle climbs along the way. See the
location
section below for a link to the proposed route map and elevation chart.
The terrain is best suited to hybrid or mountain bikes, though it can be done on a road bike. Some parts of the trail are uneven and can be muddy if wet. Please check that your bike is roadworthy before the ride, as you will be responsible for dealing with your own mechanical issues, should they arise on the day.
Weather Conditions
Cold, wet and windy weather conditions aren't conducive to sociable cycling, and part of the route has been known to flood after very heavy rain. So, if the weather forecast for the day indicates extreme conditions, the event may be postponed with as much notice as possible provided to participants.
All photos taken by OutdoorLads leaders and used with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: This is a 28 mile gentle leisure cycle. Some attendees may prefer to cycle half of the  route (14 miles) and catch a train for the other half.

Title: Cardiff Social - Part 2
Summary:
Come & join for us a night on the town in Cardiff.
This is the perfect event for members old and new to catch up with each other, find out about upcoming events and let their hair down.
We also encourage potential new members to come along and find out what we are all about. If you've not been on any of our events before, this is a great opportunity to chat to existing members and leaders about the group and to find out how friendly and welcoming we are.
If you are in any way uncertain about joining OutdoorLads, are concerned about fitness/ability or whether OutdoorLads is for you, come along, say hello, have a drink with us and judge for yourself.
We will begin our evening at the Golden Cross pub. Once the main social ends, some or all of us may well decide to head on to other bars or clubs in Cardiff.
If you have any questions before the event, please feel free to drop the Leader a message.
Join us for a night of drinks & laughter at a pub or two around Cardiff.
Want to Get Out More?
Why not check out the South Wales and South West Calendar
here
.
Photo Credits - Photos provided by OutdoorLads members
Difficulty & Distance: It's a social!

Title: Cardiff social P1 - Treetop Mini Golf
Summary:
Come and join us for a fun, relaxed and social evening.
We'll start the evening off with a game of mini golf. If you have never played before, no need to worry all abilities are welcome. We'll play a game of 18 holes, there are two challenges the Tropical Trail or Ancient Explorer. We'll be meeting at Cardiff's Tree Top Golf (roof of St David's car park -
www.adventuregolf.com/cardiff
). The price for playing is £13 per person. You don't need to book online for a spot or pay in advance, we just pay when we arrive. It's that simple.
We will then head to The Golden Cross for 21:00. I'll make a separate event for this for further details, so you can have the choice to do both or only one of the events.
Join us for a promising night of fun & games with a night on the town to a pub for drink or two around Cardiff.
Want to Get Out More?
Why not check out the South Wales and South West Calendar
here
.
Photo Credits - Photos provided by OutdoorLads members

Title: Midweek Meander - 7 Bridges and a Burger
Summary:
Join Alex for a slow mid-week walk along the Trent as it passes through the centre of Nottingham, ending up in a city centre pub for a bite to eat.
This route passes
seven bridges
, and crosses the
newest bridge
twice.  It's an out and back walk, so some of the bridges are counted twice.
We will walk from the Meadows Embankment, past Trent Bridge and loop around a lake in Colwick.
Further information about the route is available to logged in users.
If this is your first ODL event, there is information designed specifically for you on the
My First Event
page on the ODL website.
Note:
If there are no places left on this event, join the waiting list, as people frequently drop out during the week before an event, and you are likely to get a place.
Image Credits:
All images provided by the walk leader and permission is granted to ODL for their use.
Difficulty & Distance: The walk is flat the whole way but there are some ramps up over bridges.

Title: BIG20: New Forest Celebration Weekend! - Camping option
Summary:
It's OutdoorLads' 20th anniversary!
Come join us for a Summer weekend in the New Forest to celebrate this huge milestone... the BIG 20 events this year are a showcase of some of our very best events from the last 20 years, where we can enjoy a weekend celebrating all that OutdoorLads has to offer with some great activities and a few little surprises to mark the occasion, too!
We have a full weekend of events, the event pages will be bookable from 5pm on the 12th June!
Sat 18/07 09:00am -
Brockenhurst Park Run
(no event page just turn up)
Sat 18/07 11:30am -
Camp Glee, Greenwood Tree, Afternoon Tea
(walk)
Sat 18/07 10:30am -
Cultural & Heritage event - visiting Beaulieu House & Motor Museum
(paid for event)
Sat 18/07 12:00pm -
Paddle to Pub on the Beaulieu River
(paid for event)
Sat 18/07   1:15pm -
Brewery Tour
(paid for event)
Sun 19/07 10:00am -
New Forest Walk
(a shorter walk - for those in no rush to head off)
The Accommodation
This page is for camping in your tent on the camping field alongside the hostel weekend  - see the
main event page here
for full details.
For campers, the event price still includes all meals (2 x breakfasts, and evening meal Saturday night)
Dogs
: This event is not suitable for dogs.
Campervans, motorhomes, caravans:
This site accepts tents only.
Images:
Big thanks to Colin, Glenn and James (ODL Leaders) for the event photos, used with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: A weekend of events and celebration!

Title: BIG20: New Forest Celebration Weekend!
Summary:
It's OutdoorLads' 20th anniversary! Come join us for a Summer weekend in the New Forest to celebrate this huge milestone... the BIG 20 events this year are a showcase of some of our very best events from the last 20 years, where we can enjoy a weekend celebrating all that OutdoorLads has to offer with some great activities and a few little surprises to mark the occasion, too!
We have a full weekend of events, the event pages will be bookable from 5pm on the 12th June!
Sat 18/07 09:00am -
Brockenhurst Park Run
(no event page just turn up)
Sat 18/07 11:30am -
Camp Glee, Greenwood Tree, Afternoon Tea
(walk)
Sat 18/07 10:30am -
Cultural & Heritage event - visiting Beaulieu House & Motor Museum
(paid for event)
Sat 18/07 12:00pm -
Paddle to Pub on the Beaulieu River
(paid for event)
Sat 18/07   1:15pm -
Brewery Tour
(paid for event)
Sun 19/07 10:00am -
New Forest Walk
(a shorter walk - for those in no rush to head off)
Choose Your Accommodation
Bunkhouse - we have used this site a number of times and you can expect dormitory style accommodation with some spare beds available allowing us to spread-out throughout the building, there is a decent size lounge and dining area.  Showers are in a separate block 10 metres from the Bunkhouse
Camping - we have been able to secure options for camping alongside the hostel - see the
camping page
for full details.
For campers, the event price still includes all meals (2 x breakfasts, and evening meal Saturday night)
Itinerary
The following is all subject to change but should give you an idea of what to expect if this is your first ODL hostel event.
Friday
You'll be able to arrive at the site from around 4.30pm on the Friday, and find a bed in the dorms. Once settled in, there is plenty of room to relax in the lounge, with hot drinks available throughout the weekend. If your preference is for an alcoholic tipple you'll need to bring these yourself.
Friday evening will be the traditional OutdoorLads buffet. Bring along something to feed one or two people, and home-cooked is always preferred.
Once we've had a briefing over the plans for the weekend, we'll be looking for some volunteers to tidy up the kitchen and dining room, whilst the others can head back to relax in the lounge (or outside if its warm!), chat to others or perhaps enjoy some board games (don't forget to bring your favourites!)
Saturday
After breakfast on Saturday morning you'll head out on your choice of activity (we'll list these out above as they are confirmed), returning to our accommodation early afternoon. This means you will likely need a packed lunch that you will need to bring yourself.
For those of you that like your Parkrun on a Saturday morning, Brockenhurst has you covered and we'll time our daytime activities to give you a chance to do both.
OutdoorLads hostel events always rely on everyone mucking in and helping in the kitchen over the weekend, so whilst a few volunteers are busy in the kitchen, we'll hopefully have some anniversary themed activities in the lounge.
We will provide dinner for the Saturday night, so please make sure your dietary requirements are up to date. We'll also raise a glass to OutdoorLads tonight with dinner! We will need some volunteers to clear up in the kitchen and dining room, but then it's a chance to natter to fellow OutdoorLads, listen to some music and likely that there will be board games available to partake in. Being mid-Summer, lets hope it's nice and we can enjoy being outside again, too!
Sunday
Start the day with breakfast and as much tea/coffee as needed to recover from the night before, and then we'll spend an hour or so tidying up the venue and leaving it as clean as we found it, we will need to be out by midday.
Our plan is to offer a short walk on the Sunday for those that are not in a hurry to leave or you can go an explore this beautiful part of the south coast.
Good to Know....
Dogs
Sorry, this event is not suitable for dogs. Registered animals are welcome, but please let us know at the time of booking if you will be bringing an assistance dog.
Car Sharing
The is parking available but sharing is good, so if driving to the event please consider offering spare seats to other members heading the same way.
OutdoorLads Foundation
Fancy coming along but having a difficult time of it (for whatever reason), and/or finding it hard to justify the expense? Apply for a place on this event through the OutdoorLads Foundation! Nobody apart from staff members will know and we don't need lots of financial info from you either. To apply through the Foundation, you'll need to complete a very short form, which can be found along with more information
here
.
Want to Pay By Instalments?
You can arrange to pay in instalments if that helps. Please contact the office on
support@outdoorlads.com
who will help you to secure a place and set up paying in instalments. The event must be fully paid up a week before the event takes place.
Images:
Big thanks to Colin, Glenn and James (ODL Leaders) for the event photos, used with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: A weekend of events and celebration!

Title: Rowing Taster Day at Derwent Rowing Club!
Summary:
A great
opportunity to try out the sport of rowing
on the river Derwent in Derby with Derwent Rowing Club , working in partnership with OutdoorLads!
This taster day is designed to give a fun, all round introduction to rowing in a friendly, small club environment. The day is suitable for complete beginners, so come along even if you've never been in a rowing boat!
Throughout the day you will experience learning on the rowing machines, an introduction to rowing equipment and getting out on the water in a variety of boats, from learner single sculls to larger crew boats. It will be fun, relaxed, informative, and a great experience for all.
The cost for the full day is £45
, paid to the club on the day. This includes all equipment and a buffet lunch. The full day will be at least 10am-4pm. As we will be booking out a full days use of the clubs equipment and catering, please only sign up for this event if you are absolutely sure you will attend!
The leaders partner, Rob Thornton, is an OutdorLads member and has been rowing for 20 years. He is a British rowing qualified coach and vice captain and club safety officer at Derwent Rowing Club, and will be running the day for us.
Tea and coffee will be available throughout the day and a buffet lunch of sandwiches/wraps provided (please let us know of any dietary requirements in advance). The event will go ahead whatever the weather, only thunder or high winds would lead to cancellation. We do row in the rain, but we have a warm clubhouse to dry out in!
Have a look at ‘instagram@derwent_rowing_club’ to get an idea of what the club is like and what the sport of rowing looks like.
On the day you will be required to sign a temporary membership form that includes a medical declaration and swim ability question. This brings you under the clubs insurance and allows us to best keep everyone safe.
If you have any questions please contact Rob at
safetyofficer@derwentrowingclub.co.uk
You will be sent the taster day pack PDF which details the location of the club, what to bring and what to expect.
All Derwent members helping on the day are volunteers. If the event fills up very quickly and has a large waitlist we will ask more volunteers to help for the day to try and increase the event size. If we are unable to do this please look out for future events, we’d love to see you on the water!
Photos:
Photos by Rob Thornton / Derwent Rowing Club and used by OutdoorLads with permission.
Dogs
: This event is not suitable for dogs.
Difficulty & Distance: New experience and energetic, but well in the grasp of most people

Title: July North-West Social: Manchester
Summary:
Join us for the July edition of the North-West Social! We are bringing the OutdoorLads community together in the heart of Manchester for a relaxed, informal evening of conversation and connection. We’ll be meeting at one of our favourite local venues,
Via on Canal Street
, starting from
7:30pm
.
What to Expect
Whether you are a long-standing member or brand new to the group, our monthly socials provide the perfect environment to meet like-minded people, share stories from recent adventures, and brainstorm ideas for future trips. We pride ourselves on being a warm, inclusive space for everyone.
If you are new to OutdoorLads, this is the perfect time to drop by, grab a drink, and introduce yourself to the group. We understand that taking the first step to join a new social circle can sometimes feel daunting—if you have any concerns regarding fitness, ability, or simply aren't sure if OutdoorLads is the right fit for you, please come along for a casual drink. It is a fantastic opportunity to see firsthand how our members form lasting friendships and to experience the welcoming culture of our community.
While the social is officially due to end at 11pm, many of our members will stay out afterwards and may even head to some of the other iconic Gay Village venues until the early hours.
If you have any questions ahead of the evening, please feel free to message the event leader directly.
Travel and Accommodation
Getting Here:
Via is centrally located in Manchester’s Gay Village and is easily accessible by public transport. We are just a short walk from Manchester Piccadilly and Manchester Oxford Road train stations. The area is also well-served by local Metrolink tram stops and numerous city centre bus routes. For those planning to drive, there are plenty of car parks available within walking distance of Canal Street. We recommend using the
Transport for Greater Manchester (TFGM)
website or app to check the latest travel updates for your journey.

Title: Sheffield Bouldering - All Abilities (beginners welcome)
Summary:
Swap trainers for climbing shoes and join OutdoorLads for an evening of INDOOR BOULDERING at The Climbing Hanger in Sheffield.
Cards/contactless ONLY  - this venue does not accept cash
If you haven't been to The Hangar before, please click
here
to pre-register. Click on the link, scroll down, select Sheffield and follow the on-screen instructions. Completing the waiver prior to arrival will get you climbing faster.
What is Bouldering?
It's a form of climbing where instead of long high routes, the 'problems' tend to be shorter and focus on technique, balance, body positioning and sometimes power. Because of this different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, harnesses and belaying, which makes it an excellent activity for newcomers to the sport. No wonder it's often regarded as the 'purest' form of climbing.
If you're a seasoned climber you won't need convincing about the benefits of bouldering, but for those who've never climbed more than a staircase, the benefits are wide ranging: from the physical - improved strength, tone, balance, power and coordination; mental - problem solving, focus, concentration and body awareness; to the emotional - relaxation, a sense of achievement and a feeling that mind and body are working together perfectly ('feeling psyched'). On top of all this, bouldering tends to be a very social sport where you can hang out with mates and each have a go as you feel regardless of what level you climb at.
I'm new to Bouldering - can I come along?
If you have never tried bouldering before it is recommended that you have a guided session from the event leader, who will go through everything you will need to know to boulder safely before joining in with the rest of the group.
I've been Bouldering before?
As above, everyone attending the event  must abide by the centre's T&Cs etc. As an experienced boulderer you responsible for your own safety, actions and involvement at all times. As such, you should use your own judgement in taking up advice given either by OutdoorLads Volunteers or other experienced climbers.
Photos : Copyright granted to OutdoorLads by the event leader / venue.
Please note that every person participating should be aware of (and accept) that those members perceived as ‘in charge’ or leading the event are not necessarily qualified, do not need to be qualified, and are not regarded by OutdoorLads, or themselves as ‘technical experts’ but are amateur climbers with some experience and who are happy to impart their knowledge. Any advice given should be considered with this in mind by the recipient.
All those attending the event must also be aware of the centre's own terms and conditions of participation and abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre’s staff. Everyone must also take responsibility for their own safety while participating in Indoor climbing events.
Difficulty & Distance: Suitable for all abilities. If you're new to bouldering, just let the event leader know and we'll get you started!

Title: Tintern Abbey and Wye Valley Camp
Summary:
Two nights camping with OutdoorLads overlooking the Wye Valley
The Wye Valley was designated an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty in 1971 and in places not much has changed since then.   Our base for the weekend has stunning views over the valley, and is a short walk from Tintern Abbey.
The site boasts dedicated fire pits for each camping location (please do not move these and only wood purchased at site to be burnt), Toilet block with coin operated showers (£1 for 4 minutes), fridge and freezer available for use, as well as phone charging lockers.
On the Saturday you can expect a day walk leaving from the campsite, taking in the spectacular scenery of the Wye Valley and Tintern.
Awnings & Gazebo's:
An additional charge of £5 per night per awning and £7 per night per gazebo will be payable directly to the campsite if you wish to erect one.
Dogs:
Dogs are welcome on the campsite but you will be expected to be considerate of other campers and clear up behind them.  A charge of £2 per night will be payable directly to the campsite for each dog.
Campervans, Motorhomes, Caravans:
This event is suitable for tents and
SMALL
campervans only
Drones:
The use of drones is not permitted at the site.
Campfires & BBQ's
: This event will include a campfire/BBQ at the site.  Whilst our event leaders will take safety precautions there is still a risk to members. Please consider your behaviour around the campfire for your own and others safety.

Title: Lucknow Curry Night
Summary:
Join Andy and the OutdoorLads for an award-winning curry night !
Join us for a delicious evening of great food, good company, and plenty of laughter at Lucknow Junction in Farnworth.
Renowned for its warm hospitality and authentic Indian cuisine, Lucknow Junction provides the perfect setting for a relaxed and sociable night out. Whether you're a devoted curry enthusiast or simply looking to enjoy an evening with friends old and new, this promises to be a memorable gathering.
We'll come together to sample a wonderful selection of freshly prepared dishes, from rich and aromatic curries to sizzling tandoori specialities and flavourful vegetarian options. The restaurant's welcoming atmosphere makes it easy to settle in, share stories, and enjoy the simple pleasure of a meal in good company.
Beyond the food, the evening is all about friendship and conversation. It's a chance to unwind after a busy week, meet fellow members, and enjoy the camaraderie that comes from sharing a table filled with fantastic food.
So bring your appetite and join us for a relaxed evening of flavours, friendship, and fun at Lucknow Junction. Whether you're attending for the food, the company, or both, you're guaranteed a warm welcome and a thoroughly enjoyable night out.
If you fancy a drink and a catch up beforehand,
why not meet us from 7p.m. ?
Details will be sent with the event reminder email nearer the time.

Title: Bristol Balloon Fiesta
Summary:
Come and join us for a social day out to catch up with ODL members and friends whilst watching remarkable hot air balloons take flight. Watch entertainment that is being performed throughout the day and even get up close with the crew and pilots to talk with them and have a look at their ballooning equipment.
Should you choose to stay and make a full day of it, at 6pm we will witness these great balloons being prepared for flight. we'll see them inflate and watch their ascent to the skies before our very eyes.
At 9pm is the Nightglow. As the day gets darker, we will see 20 hot air balloons light up alongside the specially created soundtrack from BBC Radio Bristol.
This event is free of charge. If you wish to drive there and use their car park, you will need to pay in advance which is £5 per car. Go to the Bristol International Balloon Fiesta website for more details.

Expect a varied menu, a well-stocked bar, and plenty of conversation as we settle in for an easygoing evening together. As always, the focus is on good company, a welcoming vibe, and a simple recipe for a great night out: tasty food, great people, and plenty of laughs. 🍽️🍻😊

Title: Farnham to Tilford: Commons, Heaths & History
Summary:
A scenic Surrey stroll from Farnham to Tilford, combining riverside paths, woodland trails, open meadows, and one of the county’s prettiest villages.
Leaving historic Farnham behind, we soon swap town streets for green commons, woodland paths, riverside meadows, and sandy Surrey tracks. Following the peaceful River Wey, this varied walk takes us through beautiful countryside to the postcard-perfect village of Tilford, with its historic bridges, village greens, and charming rural character.
Highlights include:
Farnham
– A historic market town known for its medieval castle, Georgian architecture, and attractive Castle Street.
The River Wey
– A peaceful river winding through meadows rich in wildlife, including herons, kingfishers, and dragonflies.
Tilford
– One of Surrey’s prettiest villages, famous for its medieval bridges, cricket green, and the much-loved Barley Mow pub.
Wey Valley Meadows
– Protected grasslands bursting with wildflowers in spring and alive with birds and insects throughout the summer.
Dogs:
This walk is very suitable for dogs. There’s plenty of open space, river access for a drink or paddle, and several fields with livestock (dogs on leads in these areas, please).
(All pictures used from OutdoorLads with permission)
Difficulty & Distance: Distance is 17.5 km (10.5 miles); total ascent is 273 m; terrain is undulating with one significant ascent; surfaces are dirt, sand, grass and tarmac.

Title: Manchester Pride 2026 - Peel parkrun
Summary:
Join us for a Pride parkrun as we celebrate and promote
OutdoorLads
at one of the UK’s biggest and best Pride events!
🏳️‍🌈
For those of you who enjoy your weekly parkrun fix, we'll be making the most of Manchester Pride 2026 weekend and heading to Peel parkrun in Salford. If you've not heard of it, parkrun is a free, weekly, timed 5k event that takes place every Saturday at locations across the world.
We will meet at 8.45am by the parkrun sign for a group photo. I'll be wearing a red parkrun t-shirt, but feel free to wear any OutdoorLads merchandise or Pride accessories. You can register for parkrun and get your own barcode from
www.parkrun.org.uk
to scan at the end of your run to record your finish time if you wish. However, you do not need to do this to take part in the run if you don't want to.
Course description:
The course is 5k long, run on a mixture of tarmac paths and grass. This is a two lap course by the river and around a football pitch and the park. It is mainly on good quality paths except for a short section on grass, which can get muddy. The start and finish by the statue in the middle of Peel Park, near the playground.
On occasion parkruns can be cancelled due to the weather or lack of volunteers. Should this happen I will try to contact members as soon as I can.

Title: Manchester Pride 2026 - Grindr: The Opera!
Summary:
Join us as we celebrate and promote
OutdoorLads
at one of the UK’s biggest and best Pride events!
🏳️‍🌈
Grindr: The Opera! puts the most notorious gay hook-up app into the exaggerated world of opera. This musical parody takes you on a hilarious and thought-provoking journey through the world of gay dating, focusing on the greatest catalyst for the shift: GRINDR.
With catchy songs, witty dialogue, and colourful characters, this unique and ground-breaking production explores the impact of technology on human connection and the search for meaningful relationships.
Through the experiences of four gay men who use Grindr to connect with each other,
Grindr: The Opera!
offers a satirical commentary on modern gay culture.
Devon the romantic, Tom the cynic, Jack the twink and Don the daddy meet via the medium of the Grindr app, each seeking a different type of connection.
You’ll laugh, you’ll cry, and you’ll be challenged to think deeply about the ways in which technology is shaping our lives and relationships.
But above all,
Grindr: The Opera!
will make you feel alive. With its infectious energy, dynamic performances, and unforgettable characters, this production will leave you feeling inspired, entertained, and maybe even a little bit hopeful about the future of human connection.
Further information can be found on
this website
.

here

The ticket website shows the whole of the Manchester 'run', so make sure you book for the 7.30pm performance on Friday 28 August. Of course you can head along at any other time it's running either in Manchester or around the country (the discount code works for other performances and other locations I believe).

Title: “From Quills to Cobblestones: A Stratford Escape”
Summary:
Come and join us for a cultured weekend break in Stratford-upon-Avon, and enjoy fun times exploring the town's rich heritage!
City breaks are less formal and structured than our usual hostel breaks, you’ll have free time or time to do the suggested activities below, but they are all suggestions. City breaks do not have the usual Friday night buffet, (and indeed no food or drink is included in the price) however places to eat as a group will be suggested, or you may wish do your own thing or break into smaller groups.
Stratford-Upon-Avon is a renowned market town famously known as the birthplace and burial place of playwright William Shakespeare. Situated along the River Avon, this historic town draws over 2.7 million visitors annually to experience its Tudor-style architecture, the Royal Shakespeare Company theatres, and various Shakespearean heritage sites.
What’s going to happen
Itinerary:
Day 1:
Arrive late afternoon on Friday, meet & greet reception before heading off out for dinner and drinks at The Red Lion.
Day 2:
Saturday morning, Optional Breakfast at the hostel before we head into the town centre for a leisurely
city morning walk
with local guide - (£10 - not included in event price) Please book through this link
Town Walk | STRATFORD TOWN WALK
- and select Saturday 27th June at 11am.
After lunch we will take a walk along the River Avon exploring the heritage along the river. There are some potential places to visit along the river including the RSC where there is a free exhibition at the Swan theatre side of the RSC:
The Play's The Thing | Exhibition | Royal Shakespeare Company
Other potential places to visit include
Shakespeare's Birthplace
(however this is expensive at £27) and the beautifully picturesque
Anne Hathaway’s Cottage
which is a 30 minute walk away (cost of £15)
In the evening we will reconvene at the hostel before heading to an Optional Ghost tour at 7pm at a cost of £10.00 - details here:
Ghost tour
This will be followed by dinner & drinks.
Day 3:
Sunday morning, Optional Breakfast at the hostel then free time before checkout - option of a
local short walk
Accommodation
Hostel accommodation - 2 x  6 bed ensuite rooms which will only be shared with those attending the city break.
Please note that unlike most of our other hostel breaks food is not provided and we will not have sole use of the hostel, however you will only be sharing a room with other OutdoorLads members.
Dogs:
Unfortunately isn't an event for our four legged friends. Registered assistance dogs welcome, but please let us know when you book.
Photos:
pixabay users Steve Oprey, Susli and Various Photography plus users GeekyJames & Cassidyi, used by OutdoorLads with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: Weekend City Break & Social

Title: Mid-Week Evening Walk in East Yorkshire
Summary:
This is a relatively short circular walk, 6km (3.75 miles) taking around 1 and a half hours. We start and finish in the town of Pocklington. We will walk from the town up through Pocklington Woods. We will then cross the golf course and head towards the Madhyamaka Kadampa Medidation Centre, located in an idyllic setting overlooking two small lakes. We'll then descend down to the largest lake and continue back to Pocklington through Spring Wood.
This should bring us back to the town in less than 2 hours. There is a bar with an outside terrace at the golf course with nice views we can stop at for a quick drink.
Well behaved dogs are welcome on this walk.
If the event is fully booked:
please join the waiting list as people frequently drop out during the week before an event and you are likely to get a place on the walk.

Title: Picturesque walk in East Yorkshire
Summary:
Join Lee on an 8.5km (5 mile) leisurely walk over the rolling hills of East Yorkshire. The route will take us up onto the Wolds and Minster Ways. After which, there is a pub and café in the village to quench our thirst.
Total ascent is around 226m over rolling hills (gravel tracks and fields).
This walk is suitable for well behaved dogs.
If the event is fully booked:
please join the waiting list as people frequently drop out during the week before an event and you are likely to get a place on the walk.
Your First OutdoorLads Event?

My First Event
page on the OutdoorLads website.

Title: Manchester Pride 2026 - The Vigil
Summary:
Join
OutdoorLads
as we come together for one of the most moving and meaningful moments of the Manchester Pride weekend — the
Candlelit Vigil
in Sackville Gardens.
We’ll be meeting at
The Molly House from 7:30pm
, giving us time to socialise and catch-up before walking together to
Sackville Gardens by 8:30pm
for the start of the vigil.
This event is
open to all
, and you do
not
need a Manchester Pride wristband to attend.
🕯️ About the Candlelit Vigil
Hosted in partnership with George House Trust
, which has supported people living with HIV since 1985, the vigil is a powerful evening of remembrance, reflection and unity.
As the sun sets and Sackville Gardens is illuminated by candlelight, we stand together in silence to remember those we have lost to HIV and other causes, and to show support for those who continue to suffer or face persecution simply for being who they are.
This is a space for solidarity, hope and healing – a moment to reaffirm our commitment to inclusion, visibility and compassion for our LGBTQ+ siblings across the world.
Candles will be available in the gardens for a small donation to George House Trust
, who help organise and host this incredibly special event.
Whether this is your first Manchester Pride or you’ve attended many times before, the vigil offers a moment of calm and connection to close the weekend with purpose.
We hope you can join us.

Title: Manchester Pride 2026 - The Parade!
Summary:
Join us as we celebrate and promote
OutdoorLads
at one of the UK’s biggest and best Pride events!
🏳️‍🌈
We’re back with a bang this year! To celebrate 20 incredible years of OutdoorLads, we’re levelling up - we’ll have both a
float and a group of walkers
following behind as we march through the city. Expect the same high energy, bold spirit, and pride as we mark two decades of community, adventure, and friendship.
💚 The Theme:
No Place Like Home
This year, Manchester Pride celebrates the theme "No Place Like Home." For many of us, our community is the home we found for ourselves - a place of belonging, safety, and authentic connection.
OutdoorLads has been that home for thousands of people over the last 20 years, providing a space where we can be exactly who we are. Join us - whether on the float or as part of our walking contingent—as we take to the streets of Manchester to celebrate our history, our chosen family, and the unique spirit that makes our community feel like home.
📍 The Plan
We'll gather at the meeting point (Location & Time TBC) please keep an eye on your messages and this event page as this is how we'll provide updates closer to the day. We’ll be easy to spot, just look for the red OutdoorLads T-shirts (which you’ll need to wear!).
As always, we want to showcase everything that makes OutdoorLads awesome, so if you're able, get involved by bringing gear that reflects your favourite activities:
Climbers
– Wear your harness. Bonus points for clipping on nuts, hexes or cams for a bit of jingly flair!
Cyclists / Mountain Bikers
– Ride along-side on your bike. Bells and horns encouraged for extra fun!
Walkers / Hikers
– Walking poles, rucksacks, hydration bladders (contents at your discretion!) and massive OS maps are all welcome. Bonus points if you act lost and ask the crowd for directions.
We’ll also have
flags to wave
,
stickers to hand out
, and heaps of enthusiasm to share!
Most importantly, don’t forget your red OutdoorLads T-shirt!
🎟️ Parade Wristbands
To take part in the parade, you
must
collect a wristband from our muster point.
No wristband = no parade entry.
Please arrive on time, as late arrivals last year struggled to get one.
🚫
Please note:
Dogs are not allowed in the parade. This is a restriction set by the parade organisers.
🌈 After the Parade
We’ll be spending time in the Village after the parade, both to socialise and to represent OutdoorLads to potential new members.
We'll likely break into small groups to chat to the public, hand out stickers and encourage sign-ups (ideally while enjoying a few drinks in the sunshine!).
📍 Details of where to meet after the parade will be announced on the day.
Important:
You’ll need a separate
Manchester Village Pride
ticket
to access the Village after the parade. Tickets for the Gay Village Party purchased
here
.
The
parade wristband does not
grant access to the Pride event, so we recommend getting your event wristbands in advance if you plan to join us in the Village afterwards.
More details will be added here and emailed to participants as plans develop. It’s going to be another fantastic year.
We can’t wait to see you there!
🎉

Title: Manchester Pride 2026 - Picnic in the Park
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads for a picnic in the park to celebrate Manchester Pride!
We’re back!
After the fantastic success of our
2025 Picnic in the Park
, we’re thrilled to bring this event back for
Manchester Pride 2026
!
This is a fantastic opportunity to
meet new faces
,
catch up with old friends
, and ease into the celebrations before heading into the Village for the evening.
🧺 What to Bring
Your favourite picnic snacks and drinks (alcohol is allowed as the event is outside the Village cordon)
A blanket to sit on
Pride flags, face glitter, or anything else to get into the spirit!
OutdoorLads red T-shirt if you want to stand out in the crowd
No schedule or pressure – just turn up when you can and stay for as long as you like.
📍 Location
Vimto Gardens
is a peaceful green space just a few minutes' walk from both
Sackville Gardens
and the
Gay Village
. It's an ideal spot to gather before the festivities kick off.
We’ll be easy to find with our red shirts, pride flags, and picnic gear. Just look for the OutdoorLads gathering in the park!
This is a wonderful, low-key way to start your weekend, whether you’re arriving in Manchester on the Friday or just looking to enjoy some downtime with fellow OutdoorLads members before heading into the Village. No wristband required to join us for the picnic, so it’s completely open to anyone who wants to join!
We can’t wait to see you there and make more memories together at this returning favourite event!

Summary:
Polly is back and
WE
are too, for the highlight of the start of Manchester Pride Weekend!
OutdoorLads are back again taking part in New York New York's "
It’s a Gay Knockout!
". A village staple over the years.
We'll need a team of 4-6 (We can always create two teams like we did last year if we have enough people) and plenty of people to line the streets with Red OutdoorLads t-shirts and cheer us on!
Sign up and get ready to toss your handbag, chuck your sausage in the hoop or maybe don your finest heels (or whatever is left in the box of goodies) and do us proud on the high heeled egg and spoon race!
It’s a great night of laughs with your OutdoorLads friends, other communities and village friends too! So come on, get on it and let’s win this for OutdoorLads!

Title: Suppertime in Blackpool
Summary:
Join Andy and the OutdoorLads for a relaxed and enjoyable Friday evening supper in the heart of Blackpool!
This is a fun, friendly, and flexible social evening designed around whatever the group would most enjoy. Whether you're visiting Blackpool for the weekend's events that are running, or simply looking for an opportunity to meet new people, this promises to be a wonderful way to start the weekend.
We'll begin by taking a leisurely stroll along Blackpool's famous promenade, soaking up the lively atmosphere of one of Britain's most iconic seaside destinations. As we wander beside the coastline, there will be plenty of opportunities to chat, enjoy the sea views, watch the world go by, and admire the illuminations and attractions that make Blackpool such a vibrant place to visit.
After our walk, we'll head into town to find somewhere for a well-earned drink and supper. Blackpool's food scene has evolved dramatically in recent years, with a growing selection of restaurants, cafés, gastropubs, and independent eateries offering cuisine from around the world. From traditional favourites to exciting contemporary dining experiences, there is something to suit every taste and budget.
The evening is as much about the company as it is about the food. Sharing a meal together provides the perfect opportunity to relax, swap stories, make new friends, and enjoy the welcoming and inclusive atmosphere that OutdoorLads events are known for. Whether you're a regular attendee or joining us for the first time, you'll find a warm welcome and plenty of good conversation.
So come along and join us for a sociable evening of seaside strolling, great food, refreshing drinks, and excellent company as we discover some of Blackpool's newest and most exciting places to eat.
TRAVEL:
There are plenty of car parks in Blackpool, and there is free parking in the streets. A charge applies if you wish to park on the prom. Blackpool North and Blackpool South train stations are easily accessible. There are, of course, the trams which you can use.
DRESS CODE:
Smart casual clothing. No track suit bottoms please ! Shorts may be allowed in some places.
Please see below links for other events in Blackpool this weekend !
A Gay Summer Day in Blackpool
https://www.outdoorlads.com/events/gay-summer-day-blackpool-260711
A Summer Social in Blackpool
https://www.outdoorlads.com/events/summer-social-blackpool-260711
All photos taken by Growler

Title: A Summer Social in Bolton - 2026
Summary:
Come and join OutdoorLads for a summer's
Saturday
evening stroll in Bolton for our vibrant
summertime social !
We will be celebrating summer by visiting
some of the finest brew houses
, including
Ye Olde Man and Scythe
, which is thought to have been built prior to 1251 and is well renowned for its hauntings and spooky goings-on. Ye Olde Man and Scythe is full of character and has a lovely atmosphere and some fantastic regular live bands !
We will then explore the area in more detail and enjoy the fun and relaxing atmosphere. There will be plenty of opportunity for a nice ale, some nibbles, and the chance to throw some shapes on the dancefloor for those who wish to stay on a bit later.
Socials are great events for both new and existing members. If you are in any way uncertain about joining OutdoorLads, are concerned about fitness/ability, or whether OutdoorLads is for you, then please come along. Give us a go, have a drink with us and see why members have made friends through OutdoorLads. It is also a great opportunity to chat about upcoming events. These events are always friendly and enjoyed by all.
If you are interested in joining us for a meal before the event, (around 5pm ish), or if you have any other questions, please send me a message for details
.
Smart/casual dress code. Please no shorts or trackies !
Transport:
For easy access to/from Manchester and surrounding areas, the train and bus station/interchange is located in the town centre and is only a few minutes walk to the meeting point.
For more info, click
here...(https://tfgm.com/)

Title: BIRMINGHAM Indoor Climbing - MONDAY - Experienced
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads for an evening at Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre.
"EXPERIENCED" in this instance is those that have previously climbed & can answer YES to all three of these questions:
Can you fit a climbing harness correctly?
Can you tie in to a rope using the correct knots?
Can you belay another climber correctly & confidently?
Please ensure you only sign up for this event if you
satisfy all three of the prerequisites as an experienced (independent) climber!
Never climbed before? Need a refresher on the basics? Answered NO to any of the three questions above? You will need to
attend one of the beginners (supervised) events
instead, where we will happily sign you in to be supervised (two people per leader maximum), show you the ropes & teach you everything you need to know to climb safely! Beginner (supervised) events are not offered every week.
If you are climbing with us for the first time, whether Experienced or Beginner, it is essential that you send a note to the Event Leader telling them about your previous climbing experience & your goals!
Bouldering
- Most of us do roped climbing, but if you prefer to do bouldering, you can sign yourself in & go bouldering (unsupervised) instead of, or as well as, roped climbing. Please meet the leader & the group at the start though; some others might wish to do bouldering too!
Please note that we will not teach lead climbing or lead belaying on this event -
we would suggest having some
lessons in Lead Climbing
by a qualified member of staff if this is something you're interested in pursuing!
If you are subsequently unable to attend, please cancel off the event as 'no-shows' are not only rude, but also frustrating for the leader & other attendees who may have travelled over an hour away to climb with you!
Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre
Indoor climbing centres offer the challenges of climbing that you'll find on natural rock crags - simply indoors! It's a fun way to stay fit, make new friends & develop your climbing skills.
Climbing
- Unlike Indoor Bouldering there is a certain amount of skill to be mastered, such as tying in knots & belaying a partner, as well as the climbs being higher than in bouldering. If you are new to climbing or would like a refresher, then this is just for you!! If you’ve climbed before, can safely tie into a harness & can competently belay, then there’s plenty of choices of top-rope & lead climbing on offer here.
Bouldering
- Instead of tackling long high routes, the 'problems' (as they are referred to) are shorter & focus on technique, balance, body positioning & sometimes power. With the different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, so it’s excellent (but not necessarily easier) for newcomers.
Redpoint Birmingham Climbing Centre
has more than 75 lines with over 300 routes, set through some spectacular ground including its notorious 'stalactite'. It even features one of the few indoor 'Yorkshire VS cracks' in the country, to test your jamming skills. The bouldering rooms are just as interesting as its climbing routes, over 200 bouldering problems have been organised into graded circuits to provide something for everyone.
You MUST register via this event page before coming to the centre!
To climb, you will need to:
Complete a
Registration Form
(please arrive early to do this in-person the first time) & pay a Registration Fee:
Annual Registration
is
£5
or
Day Registration
is
£3
Pay an
Entry Fee
directly to the climbing centre:
Standard Entry
is
£12.50
&
Bouldering Only
is
£8.50.
Hire
climbing shoes (
£3.50
) & a climbing harness (
£3.50
) if you don't own your own.
Please see Redpoint's
website
for current prices & note that prices are subject to change.
If you have any questions or need any more information just get in touch with the event leader by clicking on their profile below, or take a look at the
My first climbing event page
for more information.
PLEASE NOTE:
All those attending the event must also be aware of the climbing centre's own terms & conditions of participation & abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre's staff.
Photos:
taken & used with permission of
R. Casabuena
&
K. Jones
.
Difficulty & Distance: This event is for EXPERIENCED (independent) climbers only!!!

Title: NOTTINGHAM Indoor Climbing (Basford) – Beginners welcome
Summary:
Join OutdoorLads at Nottingham Climbing Centre
Climbing evenings are held regularly and we tend to go to the local pub – The Lion for refreshments and a social gathering afterwards where they have a wide variety of beverages both alcoholic and non-alcoholic.
NOTE: If you are climbing with us for the first time
, please send a note to the
Event Leader
telling them about your experience and goals.
Nottingham Climbing Centre - Basford
Nottingham Climbing Centre is equipped with walls and routes to suit all skill levels and groups of climbers, whether you are a novice looking to try climbing for the first time, or an experienced lead climber, you’ll find an area to challenge you. With 28 Top Ropes routes available as well as 24 Lines of Lead climbing for the more advanced individuals. In addition, 6 auto-belays are available, so there is always the opportunity to climb, even if the numbers don’t match up. The available bouldering walls also provide that extra physical and mental challenge for those looking for something slightly different from rope work. Both Bouldering and Rope Climbing routes range from 4a – 8a.
Do I need to be Supervised as a Climber?
If you answer
NO
to any of the following three questions, then you will need to arrange to be supervised and will need to book onto a future beginners event.  If you can answer YES to all these questions, you can sign up to this event as normal:
Can you fit a climbing harness correctly?
Can you tie yourself to the end of a rope using the correct knots?
Can you belay another climber correctly and confidently?
To come along, you will need to register via this event page and to climb you will need to pay an entry fee directly to the climbing centre when you arrive, please see their
website
for up-to-date prices. We ask that all members sign up through the event page to make sure the event leader has all information of the members attending.
To climb at Nottingham Climbing Centre you will need to:
-     Complete a climbing waiver for the centre (please arrive early to do this the first time, or better still complete it online before you arrive)
-     Pay the Lifetime Registration Fee - £6.00
-     Pay an Entry Fee directly to the climbing centre - £9.50 (ensure you mention ODL)
-     Hire shoes (if you don’t have your own) - £3.50*
-     Hire a harness (again, if you don’t have your own) - £3.50*
-     Hire shoes and harness - £6.00*
*Equipment hire charges will be waived for your first visit to the centre!
Can I just come along and boulder?
Absolutely! Most of us do roped climbing, but if you prefer bouldering, you can sign yourself in and do it, as long as you can fill in the climbing centre's form correctly, watch their
safety video
and can answer the simple safety questions at reception on your first visit. Meet the group at the start; some others might be bouldering. We will make sure we come and find you when we have our tea break!
If you have any questions or need any more information just get in touch with the event leader by clicking on their profile below, or take a look at the
My first climbing event page
for more information.
Photos:
All images supplied by event leaders and used by OutdoorLads with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: Beginners welcome.

for more information.

All images supplied by event leaders and used by OutdoorLads with permission.

Title: Nottingham Bouldering (Lenton) - MONDAY - Beginners Welcome
Summary:
Swap trainers for climbing shoes and join OutdoorLads for an evening of INDOOR BOULDERING at Nottingham’s newest bouldering venue
NOTE: If you are climbing with us for the first time
, either as an experienced climber or beginner, please send a note to the
Event Leader
telling them about your experience and goals. There is a limit to the number of beginners that we can accommodate at each session. Each beginner needs to be supervised so we need to ensure there are enough experienced climbers to help you out. Climbing is great fun! It's a fab workout, in a social environment, and anyone can do it! Give it a go!
What is Bouldering?
It's a form of climbing where instead of long high routes, the 'problems' tend to be shorter and focus on technique, balance, body positioning and sometimes power. Because of this different emphasis, bouldering doesn't have the hassle of climbing with ropes, harnesses and belaying, which makes it an excellent activity for newcomers to the sport. No wonder it's often regarded as the 'purest' form of climbing.
If you're a seasoned climber you won't need convincing about the benefits of bouldering, but for those who've never climbed more than a staircase, the benefits are wide ranging: from the physical - improved strength, tone, balance, power and coordination; mental - problem solving, focus, concentration and body awareness; to the emotional - relaxation, a sense of achievement and a feeling that mind and body are working together perfectly ('feeling psyched'). On top of all this, bouldering tends to be a very social sport where you can hang out with mates and each have a go as you feel regardless of what level you climb at.
I'm new to Bouldering - can I come along?
If you have never tried bouldering before it is recommended that you have a guided session from the event leader, who will go through everything you will need to know to boulder safely before joining in with the rest of the group.
I've been Bouldering before?
As above, everyone attending the event  must abide by the centre's T&Cs etc. As an experienced boulderer you are responsible for your own safety, actions and involvement at all times. As such, you should use your own judgement in taking up advice given either by OutdoorLads Volunteers or other experienced climbers.
To climb at Nottingham Climbing Centre you will need to:
-     Complete a climbing waiver for the centre (please arrive early to do this the first time, or better still complete it online before you arrive.) You can find the
link
to the waiver and bouldering safety video
here
on Nottingham Climbing Centre's website.
-     Pay the Lifetime Registration Fee - £6.00
-     Pay an Entry Fee directly to the climbing centre - £9.50 (ensure you mention ODL)
-     Hire shoes (if you don’t have your own) - £3.50*
*Equipment hire charges will be waived for your first visit to the centre!
If you have any questions or need any more information just get in touch with the event leader by clicking on their profile below, or take a look at the
My first climbing event page
for more information.
IMPORTANT!
Please note that every person participating should be aware of (and accept) that those members perceived as ‘in charge’ or leading the event are not necessarily qualified, do not need to be qualified, and are not regarded by OutdoorLads, or themselves as ‘technical experts’ but are amateur climbers with some experience and who are happy to impart their knowledge. Any advice given should be considered with this in mind by the recipient.
All those attending the event must also be aware of the centre's own terms and conditions of participation and abide by them, along with any instructions given by the centre’s staff. Everyone must also take responsibility for their own safety while participating in Indoor climbing events.
Photos:
All images supplied by event leaders and used by OutdoorLads with permission.
Difficulty & Distance: Suitable for all abilities. If you're new to bouldering, just let the event leader know and we'll get you started!

Title: Bamford Edge Circular - DVH Way Section 1
Summary:
The Derwent Valley Trust
was set up in 1996 to promote the history, arts, wildlife and leisure opportunities that surround the river Derwent along its 55 mile course through Derbyshire. The DVH Way takes us from Ladybower Reservoir in the Peak District National Park to its end at Derwent Mouth where it flows into the River Trent near Shardlow. The Duke of Devonshire opened the walk at Chatsworth House in 2003.
The route takes us through the Chatsworth estate, Derbyshire dales, towns, villages, canals and many points of interests through the
Derwent Valley Mills World Heritage Site
. There are plenty of pubs and tea shops for refreshments along the way. The route follows the riverside path through Derby City and continues southeastwards to the historic inland port of Shardlow. Journey’s end is at Derwent Mouth where the Derwent flows into the River Trent.
The route is divided into eight sections of between  ~13 km (8 miles) and ~17 km (10.5 miles), each with a clear public transport solution. All, except the first section, are linear routes. We park at the end, travel to the start, and walk back to our cars. Sections 5, 6, 7 are brilliantly served by the East Midlands Railway Derwent Valley Line
(Derby–Matlock, hourly), which runs almost parallel to the trail the whole way. For sections (2, 3, 4 and 8), we use local buses.
Each section is a walk in itself. Join one or more, as you wish, or walk all eight sections to complete the whole route through Derbyshire. Please ask other attendees for a lift if you need one.
Section 1 — Bamford Edge Circular ~17 km (10.5 miles), total ascent 389 m (1276 ft)
The first section of the DVH Way is a scenic, wild and challenging circular route. From Hathersage we climb northwards out of the village to Bole Hill then traverse Bamford Edge - with spectacular views of Ladybower below - to reach Hatherdene, the official start point of the DVH Way. The route crosses Ladybower Dam, providing fine views of Ladybower Tor.
We continue southwards alongside the reservoir on the old Thornhill Trail - a dismantled railway - between the heights of Win Hill and Bamford Edge. The route then takes us to the riverbank for a stroll through meadows to Leadmill Bridge.
At Hathersage, the churchyard is said to be the final resting place of Robin Hood’s comrade, Little John. Charlotte Bronté stayed at the nearby vicarage in 1845. There are numerous places for refreshments and shops to browse in the village centre at the end of the walk.
The ascent is all in the first third of the route then more-or-less downhill the rest of the way. The terrain includes moorland paths at Bamford Edge then easy walking, mainly on a disused railway track, riverside paths, through woods and fields.
There is a link to the
OS Maps
route for members, below.
Well behaved
dogs are welcome
but must kept under control at all times.
Note:
If there are no places left on this event, join the waiting list, as people frequently drop out during the week before an event, and you are likely to get a place.
All photos obtained online with permission to modify use and share:
Main image: geograph.org.uk/John H Darch. Image 2: flickr.com/simon gray and commons.wikimedia.org. Image 3: geograph.org.uk/Mick Garratt. Image 4: ODL.com/Mark Butterworth
Want to keep in touch with what's going on in the Midlands / ODL Central Region?
Join our
Central Region Telegram Chat Group
, or
East Midlands Telegram Chat Group
and bookmark our upcoming events list via the short link:
https://outdoorlads.events/central
Difficulty & Distance: Distance ~17 km (10.5 miles) Total ascent 389 mts (1276 ft) over moorland paths, gritstone, disused railway track, through fields, woodland and riverside paths. Some paths are uneven and may be boggy in wet conditions.

Title: BIG Pride Camp 2026
Summary:
Join us for a BIG Brighton Pride Weekend 🏳️‍🌈🏳️‍⚧️
This is the perfect event for those that would love a great day out at Pride with a great bunch of people, and be able to return to a party with the same great people without having to navigate over-crowded bars and busy festival environments.
Brighton & Hove Pride celebrates its
35th Anniversary
on over the weekend, under the theme "The Power of Love." As the UK’s biggest Pride celebration, the city will be transformed by the spectacular Community Parade on Saturday morning, which winds from Hove Lawns through the city centre in a vibrant display of solidarity and protest.
Throughout the day, the streets of Brighton come alive with a unique, festive energy as thousands gather to honour the history of the LGBTQ+ movement. With the daytime celebrations drawing crowds from across the country, the city is the place to be for those looking to soak up the atmosphere.
Saturday
The main event on Saturday is of course Brighton Pride! 🏳️‍🌈 We're not far from the train station from where you can catch a train for a short journey into Brighton to celebrate Pride 🏳️‍⚧️.  If you want to join us on the Pride Parade through the streets of Brighton please sign up
here
.
Some of our Leaders will organise groups to head in, so we're not all cramming on to the same train.
We'll also have a small selection of other events, like local walks, for those less interested in Brighton Pride. Watch this space!
Evenings
Friday evening will have the usual social buzz as people will arrive into the evening. It's always nice to catch up in the clubhouse on the first night with people we haven't seen in a while, and meet new faces. We'll have a pub quiz on Friday for those that enjoy a little competitiveness!
Saturday night is disco night! After a full day at Brighton Pride, continue the party back in the clubhouse, where theme will be... PRIDE! 🪩🏳️‍🌈💃🏳️‍⚧️🕺🪩
Sunday night, as an optional extra night, will be a quiet affair as not everybody will choose to stay. Think board games, chill vibes, and maybe a pub quiz if there's appetite (and a host).
The bar will be open each night serving a full range of drinks, and at reasonable prices, too.
Rent-a-Tent  / Campervans, Caravans, Motorhomes
Need to borrow a tent? We'll have a small number we can rent out for the weekend.
You can rent-a-tent on this page here.
If you'd like to bring a small caravan, motorhome or campervan
,
we will
ask you to reserve a spot, here
. If there is more than one person staying in it, you each need to book a place on the main event, but only one booking for the caravan / campervan needs to be made. Please note that as this venue is not ordinarily a campsite, these are not serviced pitches, and there's no chemical waste disposal on-site - it all needs to go home with you.
Food
Meals and snacks will be served each day from the clubhouse. No need to pre-order these, just order and pay at the food counter beside the bar! Breakfasts, snacks and evening meals will be available.
You are obviously also welcome to take care of your own food by your tent, but there are
strictly no BBQs on or over the grass
(this is a rugby club first and foremost). If you're hoping to BBQ over the weekend, please only do this on a hardstanding area. Camp fires are not permitted on site at all.
Make it three nights - upgrade your Sunday!
As standard, the event is Friday and Saturday night only. However, for no extra cost you are welcome to stay on site until Monday, allowing you to make more of the area, relax after the fun of Brighton and our Pride night - and maybe partake in an epic Sunday roast dinner from the clubhouse! If there is demand, we'll put on some walks or other activities on Sunday, but there wont be a disco or other entertainment that evening - it'll be more a chatting and boardgames vibe. To give us an idea of numbers, if you are likely to stay for Sunday night, please sign up to the event page you are reading now,
as well as this page here
.
Campsite Information
The camp is at a rugby club just a couple of miles from the beach, and easily accessible by rail and road. Approx one mile from the nearest train station.
Camp Facilities
Licensed Bar
Food served every day
Hot showers
Toilets & wash rooms
Changing areas
Washing up area
Drinking water taps
Grass camping field & hardstanding caravan / campervan / motorhome area
Floodlit camping area on Friday evening for late arrivals
Camp Rules
No glass policy. This is a rugby club with pitches - this rule will be strictly enforced.
OutdoorLads has a strict zero tolerance approach to drugs. Anyone found with drugs, suspected of having taken drugs, or offering drugs will be asked to leave the site immediately, and without refund. This includes prescription drugs, other than those you have been prescribed for your own use (which are fine!)
You must respect other campers and their property.
Quiet camping between 23:00 and 07:00
- there are designated areas to socialise and talk away from peoples' tents.
Please pitch tents at least 6 feet / 2 metres apart. Any closer and it represents a fire risk.
Campervans, caravans or motorhomes only permitted if you have a booked and confirmed a campervan / caravan / motorhome place (available separately at additional cost) No awnings allowed. Maximum of 15 campervans / caravans / motorhomes, all of which must be on the designated hard standing area. You will NOT be able to arrive in such a vehicle and park it in the car park. Note - these rules are imposed by the venue.
No dogs on site - this is a rugby club with pitches. This will be strictly enforced.
No open fires or BBQs permitted on site.
You are responsible for keeping your camping area clean and tidy - please ensure it is left in the state you found it when you leave.
All photos used with permission from OutdoorLads Members.
Difficulty & Distance: Relax and chill or #GetOutMore and have an action-packed weekend!

Title: BIG Pride Camp 2026: Campervans, Caravans, Motorhomes
Summary:
Join us for a BIG Brighton Pride Weekend 🏳️‍🌈🏳️‍⚧️
This is a pitch for you to park your campervan, motorhome or caravan at the
BIG Pride Camp 2026
event. You would also need to book this if you are bringing a trailer tent or a tent box on your car.
As required by the venue, all such vehicles must be parked in a pre-defined area, which is on tarmac, and with no awnings please. There is no electric hook-up available.
Unless you have a confirmed place via this event page please do not bring a camper van, motorhome or caravan to this event, even if you will be sleeping in a tent.
You will not be able to park these vehicles in the standard car park or elsewhere on the site, because the rugby club's insurance requires such vehicles to be parked in the predefined area and the number of these vehicles on the whole site to be limited.
For those that have been before: the ambulances are no longer parked on site, so you'll not have that disturbance through the night!
